speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Well have we done, thrice-valiant countrymen,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But all’s not done. Yet keep the French the field.

speaker: EXETER
text: The Duke of York commends him to your Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Lives he, good uncle? Thrice within this hour

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I saw him down, thrice up again and fighting.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: From helmet to the spur, all blood he was.

speaker: EXETER
text: In which array, brave soldier, doth he lie,

speaker: EXETER
text: Larding the plain, and by his bloody side,

speaker: EXETER
text: Yoke-fellow to his honor-owing wounds,

speaker: EXETER
text: The noble Earl of Suffolk also lies.

speaker: EXETER
text: Suffolk first died, and York, all haggled over,

speaker: EXETER
text: Comes to him where in gore he lay insteeped,

speaker: EXETER
text: And takes him by the beard, kisses the gashes

speaker: EXETER
text: That bloodily did yawn upon his face.

speaker: EXETER
text: He cries aloud “Tarry, my cousin Suffolk.

speaker: EXETER
text: My soul shall thine keep company to heaven.

speaker: EXETER
text: Tarry, sweet soul, for mine; then fly abreast,

speaker: EXETER
text: As in this glorious and well-foughten field

speaker: EXETER
text: We kept together in our chivalry.”

speaker: EXETER
text: Upon these words I came and cheered him up.

speaker: EXETER
text: He smiled me in the face, raught me his hand,

speaker: EXETER
text: And with a feeble grip, says “Dear my lord,

speaker: EXETER
text: Commend my service to my sovereign.”

speaker: EXETER
text: So did he turn, and over Suffolk’s neck

speaker: EXETER
text: He threw his wounded arm and kissed his lips,

speaker: EXETER
text: And so, espoused to death, with blood he sealed

speaker: EXETER
text: A testament of noble-ending love.

speaker: EXETER
text: The pretty and sweet manner of it forced

speaker: EXETER
text: Those waters from me which I would have stopped,

speaker: EXETER
text: But I had not so much of man in me,

speaker: EXETER
text: And all my mother came into mine eyes

speaker: EXETER
text: And gave me up to tears.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I blame you not,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For, hearing this, I must perforce compound

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With my full eyes, or they will issue too.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But hark, what new alarum is this same?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The French have reinforced their scattered men.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Then every soldier kill his prisoners.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Give the word through.
